title: Charred and Smoky Mexican Pork Tacos
description: A delicious, pillowy dish featuring charred pork, malai, and mirepoix base, perfect for kid-friendly Mexican cuisine. Serve in tacos al pastor style for a fun twist on traditional tacos.

ingredients.name: malai paste
ingredients.notes: available at most Asian grocery stores or online

instructions: Preheat grill to 400°F. Season pork shoulder with cumin, chili powder, salt, and black pepper.
Place pork shoulder on the grill and cook for 10 minutes per side, or until charred and cooked through.
Meanwhile, heat malai paste in a pan over medium heat. Add onion and bell pepper and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic to the pan and cook for 1 minute.
Shred the cooked pork with two forks and add to the pan with the malai mixture. Stir to combine.
Warm tortillas on the grill or in a dry pan. Assemble tacos with the pork mixture, cilantro, and your desired toppings.
Serve immediately, garnished with lime wedges and avocado slices.
Optional: top with sour cream and crumbled queso fresco.
